executive_verdict.primary_failure_mode: Architecture-owned gates are green, but whole-stack certification remains blocked by external owner-loop residue or a failed independent signoff.
executive_verdict.most_urgent_fix: Do not certify whole-stack green until the external owner loop clears its live residue and independent signoff stays current.

audit_metadata.scheduler_inspection_method: direct openclaw cron list --json plus live artifact cross-checks
live_running_job_names: Push research findings to git repo

findings.title: Marketing remains externally red on outcome evidence
findings.mechanism: Marketing independent verification still fails closed because primary-repo adoption is measurement-pending.
findings.root_cause: Outcome evidence for Codeberg-primary adoption is still missing.
findings.recommended_fix: Let the marketing owner loop produce fresh measurable outcome evidence, then rerun marketing independent verification before calling the whole stack green.

findings.title: Live Gateway topology matches the current runtime state
findings.mechanism: Direct live cron inspection shows 24 enabled/total-visible jobs, 0 disabled jobs, 7 running jobs, and 1 live last-error jobs.
findings.root_cause: Architecture/runtime topology remains coherent while owner-loop work is in flight.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep direct cron inspection as the source of truth on each watchdog run and avoid conflating persisted disabled history with live runtime topology.

findings.title: Architecture verifier path is green on freshness and ownership gates
findings.mechanism: Loop integrity, health-monitor blocker localization, and shared market-intelligence consumption remain coherent after the refresh; remaining blocker classification is externalized correctly.
findings.root_cause: Architecture-side gates are healthy; remaining risk is external.
findings.recommended_fix: Rerun independent verification after each material architecture artifact refresh.

findings.title: Persisted disabled jobs remain history only, not live runtime blockers
findings.mechanism: Disabled entries still exist in jobs.json history, but live Gateway topology currently exposes zero disabled jobs.
findings.root_cause: Historical scheduler records persist after live job removal/disable cleanup.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep separating persisted disabled history from live runtime topology in every audit.

findings.title: Loop "pypi-auto-unblocker" has NO self-improvement mandate
findings.mechanism: Script UNKNOWN has no self-improvement mandate. When outcomes are flat, this loop will repeat the same tactics forever without improving or redesigning its approach.
findings.root_cause: Loop was created without a self-improvement mandate or a third-party verification requirement.
findings.recommended_fix:
Add a self_improvement_mandate section to the loop script that:
  1. Detects when outcomes are flat for N consecutive runs
  2. Triggers a redesign pass: new agents, prompt rewrites, cron changes, or path retirement
  3. Registers the loop in the self_improvement_loops.json registry with checker/runner/verifier
  4. Requires independent third-party signoff before marking the loop healthy again

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Get a fresh marketing independent pass backed by measurable primary-repo movement
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: Marketing still blocks whole-stack certification even though architecture-owned gates are coherent.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Either clears the remaining adoption blocker or preserves truthful fail-closed outcome ownership.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Keep architecture live-topology verification tied to openclaw cron list --json on every watchdog run
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: Current live inspection is clean and should remain the source of truth instead of persisted-history drift.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Stops stale topology claims from reappearing in architecture reports.

repairs_applied_this_run.change: Refreshed the audit against the current live view: 24 enabled jobs, 0 disabled jobs, 7 running jobs, and 1 live last-error jobs.
Removed stale topology mismatch as an architecture-owned blocker so any remaining red stays localized to the external owner loop.
Reconfirmed that code-backed marketing consumers still expose machine-verifiable shared market-intelligence consumption.

independent_verification.summary: Independent verification confirms the repaired architecture verifier now fails closed on stale signoff, the live loop topology/ownership checks remain green, and shared market-intelligence reuse stays machine-verifiable.
independent_verification.previous_artifact_summary: Independent verification confirms the repaired architecture verifier now fails closed on stale signoff, the live loop topology/ownership checks remain green, and shared market-intelligence reuse stays machine-verifiable.

what_still_needs_independent_verificatio: Fresh marketing independent pass backed by measurable primary-repo movement.

highest_risk_unresolved_issue.title: Marketing remains red on Codeberg-primary outcome evidence
highest_risk_unresolved_issue.why: Architecture-owned runtime checks are coherent, but marketing independent verification still fails closed because primary-repo movement is still measurement-pending.

live_running_job_names: Push research findings to git repo

notes: Architecture green here means the architecture-owned verifier path is coherent; it does not mean the whole stack is green.
Persisted disabled jobs remain history only; live disabled jobs are 0.
Independent live inspection in this snapshot saw 24 total jobs, 24 enabled, 0 disabled, 7 running, and 1 live last-error jobs.
The remaining blocker is external marketing outcome evidence, not architecture runtime drift.
No live timeout-budget repair was applied in this watchdog run.
